title: UK Government Digital Service
subtitle: Agile delivery for public digital services (GOV.UK era)
source_label: GDS service manuals, public transformation reports
context: GDS advocated user centered design, multidisciplinary teams, and iterative delivery instead of large waterfall procurements. Public write ups describe shipping alphas and betas in weeks, then improving from real user feedback.
before_summary: Large waterfall contracts, late discovery of failure, poor citizen experience.
after_summary: Small empowered teams, show working software early, continuous improvement on live services.
changes: Multidisciplinary teams: design, content, dev, policy together
Start with user needs not org charts
Alpha and beta phases before full scale
Show the thing every two weeks
Open source and reuse across departments

sprints.goal: Beta with real users on subset of journeys
sprints.notes: content design in the same sprint as engineering

sprints.goal: Iterate from analytics and user research
sprints.notes: retro actions tied to service metrics

impediments.title: Procurement rules blocked small vendor experiment

retros.action: User research slot every sprint, not only at discovery
glad: Policy and dev in the same room
sad: Too much work in progress across streams

retros.action: Definition of done includes accessibility checklist
mad: External reporting asked for waterfall dates
